TextENGNew: Until dinner, I had no desire to do anything at all. I just sat there, vacant... lost in a daze.
Had Rena's outburst left me... terrified?
...No, that wasn't it.
That had been something else -- something merely wearing Rena's face.
Then... what was it?
A feeling of pure dread.
Since that thing and Rena were separate people, I should be able to talk to Rena normally tomorrow. Right?
A strange sense of relief.
And the rational voice telling me to stop thinking, to empty my head.
...All of it churned together inside my skull, a ceaseless clamor that wouldn't quiet down.
My awareness snapped back when I heard my mother calling from downstairs.
``Keiichi~!@k@vS01/00/122700007. There's a call for you from the bookstore~.``
The bookstore?@k I couldn't think of any reason they'd be calling.
...I went downstairs and picked up the receiver.
``My apologies for calling at such a late hour.@k@vS01/11/120700120. This is Ooishi from the Okinomiya Bookshop.``
``Ooishi-san?@k@vS01/01/120100220. ...Oh, Ooishi-san, it's you!``
``Sorry about this.@k@vS01/11/120700122. Your parents picked up, so I told them I was from the bookstore.@k@vS01/11/120700123. People tend to get uneasy when they hear 'police.'``
It seemed Ooishi-san was trying to be considerate in his own way.
Even so, I didn't want my parents to catch me talking to the police.
...I switched to the cordless phone and hurried back to my room on the second floor.

``Sorry again for the late call.@k@vS01/11/120700125. Actually, the phone number I wrote down for you yesterday turned out to be an old one.@k@vS01/11/120700126. My sincere apologies.@k@vS01/11/120700127. Would you mind jotting down the correct number?``
He gave me his direct office number, and I wrote it down.
I thought that would be the end of it, but he launched into idle chatter, clearly in no hurry to hang up.
``So then, Maebara-san.@k@vS01/11/120700129. Has anything unusual happened?``
...I see.@k So this was the real reason for the call.
I was momentarily speechless at his roundabout, adult way of steering the conversation.
``Ooishi-san... are you from around here?``
``Yes, I am.@k@vS01/11/120700131. Born and raised in Okinomiya.``
If he was a local... he might know.
``Um, Ooishi-san.@k@vS01/01/120100225. ...Do you know what 'Onikakushi' means?``

``Ah... that would be when someone is spirited away by demons -- vanishing without a trace.@k@vS01/11/120700133. It's an expression unique to this area.@k@vS01/11/120700134. Same meaning as 'kamikakushi' -- what most people call being spirited away.``
The very thing my friends refused to tell me, this older man answered without hesitation.
...His candor, his willingness to answer openly, made me just a little bit happy.
``Hinamizawa was... hmm, I'm not sure if I should be telling you this, Maebara-san.``
``Don't hold back on me now.@k@vS01/01/120100227. If you won't talk, then neither will I!``
``Ah, no, no!@k@vS01/11/120700137. That's not what I meant. I just... didn't want to upset you.@k@vS01/11/120700138. ...You see, long ago, Hinamizawa was feared as the village where demons dwell.``
``Demons?@k@vS01/01/120100229. You mean the kind with iron clubs in the underworld?``
``Hmm~ more like man-eating demons, really.@k@vS01/11/120700140. There are old tales about them coming down to the village, snatching people away and devouring them whole. That sort of thing.``
This act of demons spiriting people away was what 'Onikakushi' originally referred to.
``...You mentioned the curse and the demoning away happen together.@k@vS01/01/120100231. ...What exactly does that mean?``
I already knew about the mysterious deaths occurring five years running.
...But I hadn't heard that people had also been vanishing each of those five years.
``The curse and the demoning away always happen together?@k@vS01/11/120700142. That's the first I've heard of it.@k@vS01/11/120700143. Is that really the case, Maebara-san?``

``That's what I want to know.@k@vS01/01/120100233. ...Rena and Mion were talking about it. They said that if it's truly Oyashiro-sama's curse, then both the death and the demoning away always occur.``
Ooishi-san fell into a low grumble on the other end of the line.
...Had something occurred to him?
``Maebara-san.@k@vS01/11/120700145. You know about the first incident, correct?``
``Yes.@k@vS01/01/120100235. One of the six perpetrators is still on the run, right?``
``Take that, for instance.@k@vS01/11/120700148. ...What if he wasn't on the run, but was demoned away?``
Huh?!@k ...That was an astonishingly bold hypothesis from Ooishi-san.
The incident four years ago had been a particularly heinous crime.
The perpetrators had already been identified.
The police printed massive numbers of wanted posters with his face plastered on them.
They had eyes everywhere, tightening the net on every possible escape route.
Yet... four years later, not a single lead.@k ...Assuming the police weren't incompetent.
Such a bold hypothesis... I couldn't simply laugh it off.
``Then what about the following year?@k@vS01/01/120100237. The man who supported the dam project and his wife both died in an accident, right?``

``Well, actually.@k@vS01/11/120700150. ...Officially, only the husband died in the accident.@k@vS01/11/120700151. His wife's body was never recovered.@k@vS01/11/120700152. ...Under current law, without a body, she's classified as missing.``
At the time of the incident, the river below the cliff had swollen into raging, muddy rapids.
Police divers searched every stretch of the main river and its tributaries for dozens of kilometers, but in the end, the wife's body was never found.
``But... isn't that just the body not being found? She's still dead, isn't she?@k@vS01/01/120100239. That's different from being demoned away...``
``Without a body, we can't declare someone dead.@k@vS01/11/120700154. By law, she's treated as alive until a set number of years have passed.``
I wasn't sure whether this qualified as being demoned away...
The wife was missing.
That was the only certainty.
``What about the third year?@k@vS01/01/120100241. The shrine priest died of illness.@k@vS01/01/120100242. And his wife committed suicide...?``
changeReason: Changes numbers: old has {'3'}, new has set().

TextENGNew: ``Maebara-san.@k@vS01/11/120700156. It's actually the exact same story.``
The wife had apparently thrown herself into a bottomless swamp deep in the woods around Hinamizawa.
...In other words, there was only circumstantial evidence.
Just a suicide note and a pair of sandals neatly placed at the swamp's edge...
...Divers had recovered several of her belongings from the swamp, but the body itself was never found.
The investigation headquarters, suspecting a staged suicide, was still searching for her as a person of interest.
``I can't say for certain whether these cases qualify as being demoned away.@k@vS01/11/120700158. But just as you said, every year, one person does go missing.``

``This year, the woman who was with Tomitake-san has gone missing...@k@vS01/01/120100244. ...Then what about last year, when the housewife was beaten to death?@k@vS01/01/120100245. Did someone go missing then too?@k@vS01/01/120100246. ...I heard the killer was arrested, right?``

``Yes, he was arrested.@k@vS01/11/120700160. He already had a criminal record.@k@vS01/11/120700161. He confessed to the crime while being questioned about a separate incident.@k@vS01/11/120700162. ...But here's the thing.@k@vS01/11/120700163. Shortly after his arrest, a child from the victim's household went missing.``
``Whether the disappearance was crime-related is unclear...@k@vS01/11/120700165. The investigation is still ongoing.``
``But the killer was already caught, right?@k@vS01/01/120100248. Was there an accomplice?!``

``Who knows... it was most likely a lone perpetrator.@k@vS01/11/120700167. Problem is, we can't verify anything now.@k@vS01/11/120700168. ...The man died in his holding cell during the investigation.``
He had choked to death on a spork from a meal tray, lodged in his throat.
Whether it was suicide or an accident remained unclear.
``So... for the past five years, there has always been one death and one disappearance...?``
``That's correct.@k@vS01/11/120700170. ...Actually, I'm surprised myself.@k@vS01/11/120700171. I never noticed that common thread.``
I didn't think this would be the key to solving the cases.
...It was merely a shared pattern, nothing more.
``...Could it be... that the people who were demoned away... have something in common?``
Ooishi-san let out a long, contemplative hum.
I took it upon myself to organize the facts.

``Year one was the dam construction worker.@k@vS01/01/120100252. Year two, the wife of the dam project supporter.@k@vS01/01/120100253. Year three, the shrine priest's wife.@k@vS01/01/120100254. Year four, a child from the victim's household. And year five, the victim's companion... I suppose.@k@vS01/01/120100255. ...There doesn't seem to be an obvious connection.``
``Setting the first year aside, wives and companions... those do stand out.``
...He was right. There did seem to be a lot of them.
If that was the pattern... then the fourth year, with the victim's child, was the outlier.
Married couples seemed to be the dominant pattern, yet this one case involved a parent and child instead.
...Come to think of it... I was fairly sure Tomitake-san had mentioned something like 'the younger brother is alive and moved away.'
``The child who went missing in the fourth year... what were they like?``
``From what I hear, he was a quiet type.@k@vS01/11/120700174. One year older than you.``
``His name was Satoshi Houjou.``
``...Huh? Satoshi...?!?!``
That name rang a bell.
...Hadn't they told me that Satoshi transferred out last year...?!
``He attended your school until last year.@k@vS01/11/120700177. You haven't heard about him?``
Now that he mentioned it... when I first transferred in and was shown my seat, I seemed to recall being told it 'belonged to a student who recently transferred out'...?
Then... the seat I'd been sitting in... belonged to the one who was demoned away... a boy who vanished?!
I recalled the cold, clammy feel of that desk's surface... and a chill ran through me...
The string of mysterious deaths -- no, Oyashiro-sama's curse -- had finally connected to me.
That chilling sensation against my fingertips... it was as if Oyashiro-sama had reached out and stroked the back of my neck...
``...Oyashiro-sama's curse... huh?``
...Could Oyashiro-sama's curse actually be real?
I'll confess honestly.
...I was starting to believe in Oyashiro-sama's curse.
And it terrified me.
...That was precisely why I wanted to believe it was a conspiracy, some human plot -- anything but a curse.
But... no matter how much I investigated, I found no hint of that.
No -- the more I dug, the more bewildering it all became.
...If I kept going... eventually... I might stumble upon something I was truly never meant to know...
To live on in blissful ignorance, or to press deeper in pursuit of an answer I might regret...@k ...Which would truly make me happier?
It was possible... that next year's curse would fall on me.
...A stay of execution lasting only one year.
...That was when I remembered.@k ...Rena.
Rena had declared that she would be the next victim of the curse.

``...Ryuugu Rena-san, you say?@k@vS01/11/120700179. She transferred in last year. Your classmate, correct?@k@vS01/11/120700180. ...These are rather disturbing incidents for a young girl.@k@vS01/11/120700181. It wouldn't be strange for her to be frightened, would it?``
It wasn't that simple.
She had stated plainly that she would be next.
She spoke as though she had a specific, concrete reason to believe it.
``...It felt more like certainty than fear.@k@vS01/01/120100260. ...How should I put it... it was...``
Rena's terror.
Rena's transformation.
Someone who resembled Rena, but wasn't Rena at all...
...Was that connected to this?
All the unease I'd felt toward Rena throughout the day came flooding back.
``I see...@k@vS01/11/120700183. I'll look into it on my end.@k@vS01/11/120700184. In the meantime, Maebara-san, please keep an eye on Ryuugu-san for me.``
``...You mean... you want me to spy on Rena?``
``That's not what I mean at all, Maebara-san.@k@vS01/11/120700186. I just want you to watch out for your friend -- make sure she doesn't become the next victim.``
I was genuinely impressed by his smooth, adult way of phrasing things, and was about to fall silent when--
Knock knock!
The sudden rapping at my door nearly sent my heart through my ribs.
Reflexively, I hid the phone receiver for no good reason.
``Keiichi~, open the door for me~.``
Dad's oddly cheerful voice came from the other side.@k ...What did he want?
And at this hour, too...
I'd been sitting in the same position throughout the entire phone call, so when I stood up, my joints ached.
When I opened the door, there was my father, standing with a tray balanced in both hands.
On the tray were cookies and two teacups of black tea -- a rare luxury in our house.
Complete with sugar cubes and lemon slices. The full treatment.
...No matter how you looked at it, this was suspiciously generous.
``Wh-what is it, Dad?@k@vS01/01/120100265. ...What's all this about??``
``Oh come now, don't play coy, Keiichi~.@k@vS01/00/122600005. I'm coming in~.``
Dad was grinning ear to ear, practically beaming.
But... in all my life, I'd never been treated to service like this.
...What on earth had gotten into him?
``...So, what were you two talking about?``
I flinched.
...It wasn't something I needed to hide from my father, but...@k ...How was I supposed to explain that I'd been on the phone with a police detective (if that's what he was) this late at night?
``N-nothing, really...@k@vS01/01/120100267. Just a friend...!``
``I'm not talking about the phone call.@k@vS01/00/122600008. She was just here, wasn't she?@k@vS01/00/122600009. Rena-chan.``
``...She wasn't here, though?``

``Oh, stop pretending~!@k@vS01/00/122600011. Rena-chan came over to visit just now.@k@vS01/00/122600012. You two seemed to be chatting for a while, so I thought I'd bring up some tea, but it looks like I just missed her.``
I couldn't make sense of what Dad was saying.
...And yet, an icy sweat slithered down my spine.
``H-how... how long were we supposedly talking...?``
``Rena-chan came upstairs around half past... so maybe about an hour?``
``You saw her... go up to the second floor?``
``Yeah, I did.@k@vS01/00/122600015. I even told her your room was the door at the end of the hall upstairs.``
Rena had come to my house about an hour ago.@k ...Dad had greeted her at the door... and called up to me on the second floor.
"Rena-chan went upstairs at about half past... so it was close to an hour, I guess?"
Since I hadn't answered but Dad knew I was home, he'd invited Rena inside.
...And told her my room was upstairs.
...Rena had thanked Dad and headed up the stairs...
I must have been so absorbed in my phone call with Ooishi-san that I didn't hear...
...Then she'd passed Dad on his way up with the tea, and went home.
She came upstairs. Close to an hour passed.
Then she passed by Dad and left.
Th-then... after she came upstairs... and before she went home... where was Rena?
Between my room and the staircase, there's nothing but a narrow hallway.
In other words... Rena had... for nearly an hour... been standing in the hallway... no... standing right outside my door the entire time...
My door is flimsy.
Voices from inside would carry through almost perfectly.
Between my room and the staircase... there was only a short hallway.
``Honey, don't tease the boy.@k@vS01/00/122700009. ...More importantly, come clean up your studio.@k@vS01/00/122700010. You're going to spill something again.``
Mom called Dad from downstairs.
With a disappointed smile, Dad left the tray behind and headed back down.
......All the disturbing, careless things I had said to Ooishi-san raced through my mind......
"Dear, don't tease him too much.@k@vS01/00/122700009. ...More importantly, clean up your studio.@k@vS01/00/122700010. You're going to spill again."
In this dim, shadowy hallway... the entire time...?
What had she seen?
What had she heard?
Just now... while I was talking to Ooishi-san... behind my back, a mere 200 centimeters away... Rena had been standing there... the whole time...?
The faint wisps of steam from the two teacups twisted into ominous shapes as the scent of black tea slowly filled my room...
What had she seen?
What had she heard?
Why had she come?
The faint wisps of steam from the two teacups twisted into ominous shapes as the scent of black tea slowly filled my room...
